body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
We want to be able to construct vertical domain (Point and Column) that include latitude and longitude. This will be helpful e.g. when we want to read in spatial maps of data or parameters and select the data nearest to the lat/lon of a simulation on these domains.


## To-do
- [x] add Point constructor
- [x] add Column constructor
- [x] add Point test
- [x] add Column test
- [x] check Point AxisTensor construction (is identity matrix ok?)
- [x] add getter functions for lat, lon
- [x] test getter functions for lat, lon


## Content
<!---  specific tasks that are currently complete 
- Solution implemented
-->

title: add lat/lon constructors for Point and Column

body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
The medlyn term is = 1+g1/sqrt(VPD). When VPD is zero or positive, this errors. As suggested by @trontrytel, we could compute the inverse of this, and then compute the stomatal resistance, but all of our names everywhere are "conductance" so this would be a somewhat big change to make. For now, I clipped VPD to be > 1e-2 Pa. This makes sqrt(VPD) 1000x smaller than g1 which seems reasonable, as g1~100. This leads to a conductance which is very large (i.e., a very small stomatal resistance) as we expect.
